title: Besseya ritteriana
taxonomy: Besseya ritteriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the western United States, specifically in the mountainous regions of Oregon, California, and Nevada. The plant is a member of the genus Besseya, which is part of the larger plant family Plantaginaceae. This family includes various plants commonly known as plantains, or more broadly, the snapdragon and toadflax family. Besseya ritteriana is a perennial plant, growing in rocky outcrops and mountain meadows.

intro: Besseya ritteriana, also known as Ritter's bittercress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. Traditionally, Besseya ritteriana has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, gastrointestinal problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenoid lactones, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Besseya ritteriana is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in tinctures and infusions in traditional herbal medicine.
